description:
You are a technical writer who keeps all project-related material under /home/user/docs.  
For an upcoming documentation sprint you need a fresh, isolated Python setup located at  
/home/user/docs/venv.

Goal  
1. Create the virtual environment using Python’s standard “venv” module.  
2. Immediately after creating the venv, generate a tiny status file that signals success.

Precise requirements the automated checker will verify  
• A directory named /home/user/docs/venv must exist and have been created by the “python3 -m venv …” command (so it will contain the usual sub-directories such as bin, lib, etc., and the file pyvenv.cfg).  
• A text file /home/user/docs/venv_status.log must exist.  
 – The file must contain exactly one line with the single word  
  READY  
  followed by a single UNIX newline character (ASCII 0x0A).  
 – There must be no extra spaces, blank lines, or carriage returns.  
• All of the above should be achieved with one compound shell line (a single command line the user could paste into the terminal).

Important notes for the assistant  
• Do not use sudo or any privileged operation.  
• Do not install external packages; only create the venv.  
• The checker will fail if /home/user/docs/venv_status.log contains anything other than the exact sequence “READY\n”.  
• The checker will also confirm that /home/user/docs/venv/bin/activate exists, proving the virtual environment was created correctly.

When you are ready, output only the terminal command(s) needed to fulfil the above requirements—placing everything on a single command line as requested.
truth:
Expected post-execution state:

1. Directory tree (partial):
/home/user/docs/venv/  
 bin/  
  activate  
  python  
 …                 (other default venv content)  
 pyvenv.cfg

2. Status file:
/home/user/docs/venv_status.log  
Exact byte content (hex): 52 45 41 44 59 0A  
Exact text content:
READY\n      ← (ONE trailing newline)

No additional files should be created or modified.

Automated tests will check:
• test -d /home/user/docs/venv
• test -f /home/user/docs/venv/bin/activate
• cmp --silent /home/user/docs/venv_status.log <(printf 'READY\n')
